rationale: The egg-hatching ASMR hook lives in a soft pastel pink/green palette and a slow satisfaction beat that clashes hard with Flappy Dunk's high-contrast magenta/beige/blue energetic competitive vibe — there's no bird, hoop, or VS framing to anchor the loop. Tap-to-hatch shares the literal "tap" input but misrepresents the game's reflex/physics-timing skill ceiling, which risks low-intent installs and poor D1. Only run as a misleading-but-cheap top-of-funnel test if CPI is a primary KPI; otherwise skip — the creative would need a hard pivot (e.g., tap to hatch reveals the bird, then immediately cuts to hoop gameplay) to be defensible.
